title: UK Government APIs persona, story and interaction journeys
description: Traceability from seven shared personas and nineteen user stories in the Explorer and geospatial manuals to the 100-question suite, plus deterministic browser journeys and focused Playwright coverage for interaction behaviour the retrieval questions cannot exercise.

personas.name: Policy researcher
personas.need: Find relevant public-sector APIs or data sources and understand their provenance.

personas.name: Data engineer
personas.need: Judge whether endpoints, protocols, licences, contracts and access metadata support reuse.

personas.name: Service assessor
personas.need: Check that controls, gaps and quality claims are understandable and accessible.

personas.name: Knowledge curator
personas.need: Check whether the pack supports useful search, facets, graph, timeline, map and resource inspection.

personas.name: AI operator
personas.need: Understand corpus boundaries and obtain source-grounded catalogue evidence.

personas.name: Spatial data analyst
personas.need: Find coordinates, spatial files and ArcGIS/OGC services, then inspect bounded geometry without losing source context.

personas.name: Area-based policy analyst
personas.need: Find statistics, reports and services about named areas even when source geometry is absent.

stories.user_story: Start with the overview so the pack boundary, scale and notices are clear before searching.
Search by provider, domain, protocol or known term and receive relevant, explainable results.
Filter and sort a reduction while retaining inspectable URL state and honest result counts.
Inspect a record card with provenance, access, licence and metadata-quality context while gaps remain explicit.
Explore graph relationships without losing the selected target and inspect a selected edge.
Review dated records in chronological context without treating catalogue dates as operational currency.
Compare record types so API products, operations, data products and capability documents are not conflated.
Inspect resources, endpoint hosts, formats, documentation and evidence inherited from parent records.
Treat a small result count as a boundary of this pack rather than a claim about the wider world.
Reduce the current context by explainable spatial evidence or UK coverage, select a mapped record and retain the Map reduction in the public URL.
Reduce Map and the shared Explorer context by evidence class, recognised UK area or other declared coverage.
Distinguish source coordinates, representative centroids, marker clusters and spatial records that cannot be located.
Select a marker or Map record while retaining the ordinary route, detail card and provenance context.
Preview direct GeoJSON only after an explicit action and see bounded feature, coordinate, response and WGS84-bounds metadata.
Discover the first feature layer from an ArcGIS service root and request a capped GeoJSON preview.
Recover from linked-only formats or failed preview requests without losing local evidence or the external source route.
Share and revisit Map reductions through view changes, reload and browser Back/Forward using public URL state.
Use Map with named controls, keyboard marker activation and a narrow-screen single-column layout.
Author source-backed geography metadata that preserves code, vintage, CRS, boundary variant and derivation.

journeys.title: Search, filter, sort and restore URL state
Select and highlight a graph relationship, then drag its drawer
Reduce geospatial results on the Map and inspect a record
